title: Open Source AI In 17 Minutes
description: Learn what open-source AI is, why it matters, and how to run powerful models locally with Ollama.

cards.title: Welcome to Open Source AI
cards.body: You'll learn what open-source AI is, why it's taking over (hint: it's way cheaper!), and how to run powerful models locally on your own computer. No cloud required!

cards.title: What is Open Source AI?
cards.body: Open source AI means the model's core components are publicly available — architecture, weights, training code. You can use, modify, and redistribute them freely.

analogy.text: Think of it like an open book recipe vs. a secret sauce. You can see exactly how it's made and adapt it to your needs.

bullets: Model architecture and weights are public
Contrast to closed models like GPT, Claude, Gemini

source.author: Tina Huang
source.description: Defining open source AI vs closed source

cards.title: Why Open Source AI is Taking Over
cards.body: Since DeepSeek R1 in January 2025, open source models finally compete with the best closed models. The advantages are massive:
bullets: Privacy: Your data stays on your machine
cards.safe: Chinese models dominate the leaderboards, but since they're open source, you run them locally — no external servers involved!

cards.title: Quick check
cards.question: What's the main advantage of open source AI over closed source models like GPT?

options.text: You can run them locally with full control and lower costs

cards.correctFeedback: Exactly! Cost, privacy, and control are the killer advantages.
cards.wrongFeedback: Not quite. The main win is cost, privacy, and control — you can run them locally without API fees.

cards.title: The Trade-offs You Should Know
cards.body: Open source models aren't perfect yet. Here's what to expect:
bullets: Setup complexity: More steps than clicking a web app
Fewer bells & whistles: Less polished out of the box
DIY maintenance: You manage security and updates
cards.safe: The gap is closing fast! Quantization tech now lets you run powerful models on regular PCs.

cards.title: Top Models to Watch
cards.body: The leaderboard keeps changing, but here are the current champions:
bullets: DeepSeek R1 — the model that changed everything
cards.safe: Don't memorize these names! We'll show you how to find the latest top performers.

cards.title: Find the Best Models
cards.body: Before downloading anything, check the current rankings and try models online:
bullets: Visit lmsys.org/arena to see live model battles
Try models directly in the browser before committing
cards.safe: These leaderboards update constantly as new models are released. Always check before choosing!

cards.title: Halfway There!
cards.body: You now understand what open source AI is and why it's exploding. Next up: actually running these models on your own computer!

cards.title: Meet Ollama: Your Model Manager
cards.body: To run models locally, you need a model manager. Ollama is the most popular choice — think of it as an app store for AI models.

analogy.text: Just like how your phone's app store downloads and manages apps, Ollama downloads and manages AI models on your computer.

bullets: Works on Mac, Windows, and Linux
Provides simple commands to run models

source.author: Tina Huang
source.description: Introduction to Ollama and model managers

cards.title: Install Ollama
cards.body: Get Ollama running on your computer:
bullets: Click the download button for your operating system
Run the installer and follow the prompts
Open your terminal/command prompt to verify
cards.codeCaption: You should see version info if installed correctly

cards.title: Download Your First Model
cards.body: Let's download Llama 3, one of the most popular models:
cards.codeCaption: This will download several GB — be patient! The model is being stored locally on your machine.
cards.warn: Large models (7B+ parameters) can be several gigabytes. Make sure you have enough storage space!

cards.title: Run Your Local AI
cards.body: Now for the magic moment — start chatting with your local AI:
cards.codeCaption: You'll see a prompt where you can type questions. Type 'exit' or '/bye' to quit.
cards.safe: Your conversations are completely private — nothing leaves your computer!

cards.title: Quick check
cards.question: If your PC struggles to run a large model, what should you look for?
cards.correctFeedback: Right! Quantized models are compressed versions that run on modest hardware.
cards.wrongFeedback: Try quantized models! Look for Q4 or Q8 variants — same model, smaller footprint.

cards.title: Hardware Struggling? Try Quantization
cards.body: If models run slowly or crash, quantized versions are your friend:
bullets: Same model intelligence, less memory usage
Perfect for regular laptops and PCs
cards.codeCaption: The 'q4_0' suffix means it's quantized to 4-bit precision

cards.title: Build Your First Agent
cards.body: Connect Ollama to agent frameworks for automation:
bullets: Connect to Open WebUI for a ChatGPT-like interface
All work with Ollama's local models
cards.safe: Since you're running locally, there are no API costs for your automations!

cards.title: The Four Model Sizes
cards.body: Models come in different sizes based on parameters. Choose based on your hardware:
cards.safe: Start small! A 7B quantized model can be surprisingly capable on most modern computers.

cards.title: The Future is Bright
cards.body: Open source AI is rapidly improving:
cards.safe: You picked the perfect time to jump in. The tools are finally mature enough for everyone!

cards.title: You're Now an Open Source AI User!
cards.body: You've learned what open source AI is, why it's taking over, and how to run powerful models locally with Ollama. Your data stays private, costs stay low, and possibilities are endless!
